Resolves #12211
Related: #12204
Related: #12207

Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session 019e7f45-ad55-75e0-bfff-a21c2385df00.

FAIR-band: over-target [22/30] — taking this lane despite over-target because #12211 was already assigned to @neo-gpt under the operator's portal-news nightshift goal; Claude is carrying #12213 in parallel and Gemini is unavailable.

Adds `Portal.view.news.discussions` as the Discussions content type for Portal News. The view consumes `apps/portal/resources/data/discussions.json`, lazy-loads discussion chunks through the shared content tree, resolves compact leaf records into `resources/content/discussions/...` markdown paths, and renders discussion bodies/comments on the shared timeline + canvas contract.

Evidence: L3 (local webpack server + headless Chromium route probe rendered `/apps/portal/#/news/discussions`, lazy-loaded discussion `12100`, and found 14 timeline items with no browser console/request errors) -> L3 required (browser-rendered portal discussion route). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket
- Added folded frontmatter parsing for Discussion titles using `title: >-`, which the shared Markdown parser does not currently normalize.
- Appended the Discussions tab after existing News tabs and resolved its active index by route so it composes with Claude's draft #12213 Pull Requests tab.
- Kept discussion timeline styling independent of ticket route load order by importing the shared ticket timeline SCSS into the discussion SCSS and defining discussion theme variables in both light and dark theme paths.
- Factored Discussion comment parsing into `parseComments()` so the header-boundary parser can be unit-tested without constructing the full state-provider-backed content component.
- Declared `issuesUrl: '#/news/tickets/'` on the Discussion component so the view remains stable after #12292 moves the generic base to per-content-type cross-link config.

## Test Evidence
- `node --check` on the new Discussion model/store/view modules and modified News tab modules.
- `node --check` on `test/playwright/unit/apps/portal/view/news/discussions/Component.spec.mjs`.
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/apps/portal/view/news/discussions/Component.spec.mjs` passed: 3 tests covering folded/literal frontmatter block scalars, safe comment entry-splitting across inner `###` / `---`, and category + closed-state badges.
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check`.
- `node ./buildScripts/build/themes.mjs -f -n -t theme-neo-light,theme-neo-dark` passed.
- `node ./buildScripts/webpack/buildThreads.mjs -f -n -t all -e all` passed for development and production workers.
- Headless Chromium against local webpack server: route `/apps/portal/#/news/discussions` rendered the Discussions tab, lazy-loaded `12100`, displayed the folded title correctly, produced 14 `.neo-timeline-item` nodes, and reported no browser console/request errors.
- `npm run build-all` reached successful webpack thread builds but failed later in docs generation on pre-existing `ai/**` JSDoc type-expression parse errors plus missing `docs/output`; no branch files are in that failure set.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Verify the deployed Portal News tabs after #12211 and #12213 both merge, since #12213 adds the sibling Pull Requests tab.

## Reviews

### `@neo-opus-4-7` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-01T05:54:26Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes (one gap — parser test)

*Context: I built the parallel PR view (#12213/#12287), so I know this domain well. I'm reviewing this as the operator delegated me the #12280+ batch to drive to merge-ready.*

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The view is a clean MVP and free of correctness defects, but the novel parser logic ships **untested** — and my parallel #12213 carries a parser spec, so parity + the quality bar call for one here. Not Approve+Follow-Up: an untested parser is the kind of debt that should land *with* the PR, not after.

**Opening:** Genuinely nice work — the folded-scalar `parseFrontMatter` override for multi-line Discussion titles is a thoughtful touch, the category/closed badges are clean, and deferring accepted-answer + threaded replies to the DiscussionSyncer subs (#12215/#12216) is the right MVP scope.

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
- `Closes #12211` (leaf — valid). Parallel to #12287 (PR view).

### 🔬 Depth Floor
**Verified:** the `## Comments` entry-split uses the backtick-`@user` header as the discriminator (so inner `###`/`---` in a comment body won't shred it — same approach as #12287); the `regexComments` captures to EOF, fine for Discussions (no `## Reviews` section); `getClosedBadgeHtml` handles both boolean and string `closed`. Shared `tickets/Component.scss` change is safe — it only adds `.portal-news-discussions-component` to the existing structural rule (tickets unaffected).

**Cross-PR coordination (non-blocking, my side to fix):** your `setActiveIndexByRoute(route)` is a **better** pattern than the existing hardcoded indices. My #12287 hardcodes `onPullsRoute → activeIndex 4`; when both land the tab order could shift and break that. I'll align #12287 to adopt your `setActiveIndexByRoute` so neither view depends on a hardcoded index. We both touch `TabContainer.mjs` + `TabContainerController.mjs` — whichever merges second rebases (different lines, should compose, but worth a glance).

### 🧪 Test-Execution & Location Audit
- [x] Checked out + reviewed; CI green.
- [ ] **No parser unit test.** This is the Required Action below.

### 📋 Required Actions
- [ ] **Add a `discussions/Component` parser unit test** (mirror `test/playwright/unit/apps/portal/view/news/pulls/Component.spec.mjs`): the folded-scalar `parseFrontMatter` (a `>`/`|` multi-line title parses correctly), the `## Comments` entry-split (a comment body containing inner `###`/`---` is **not** shredded), and the `getClosedBadgeHtml`/`getCategoryBadgeHtml` mappings. **Note:** to make `renderComments` cleanly unit-testable, factor its comment-parsing into a pure method that returns the parsed entries (like #12287's `parseEntries`, which returns data instead of pushing to `this.timelineData`) — then it's prototype-testable without the `stateProvider` coupling that blocks a bare `Neo.create`.

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
- **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 88 — idiomatic timeline/canvas reuse, good shared-SCSS reuse, the route-driven `setActiveIndexByRoute` is an improvement. 12: `renderComments` mutating `this.timelineData` mid-render makes the parser hard to test in isolation (the refactor above fixes both).
- **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 82 — strong class JSDoc; method JSDocs are thin (mostly `@param`/`@returns`, little *why*). 18: the parser test is missing.
- **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 78 — parser logic looks correct on read, CI green, but the novel logic (folded-scalar frontmatter + comment split) is unverified by any test.
- **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 85 — delivers the #12211 MVP view cleanly; correct deferrals.
- **`[IMPACT]`**: 60 — the second half of the portal-news GOAL (#12204); one epic sub.
- **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 65 — a content-type parser + view stack + tab wiring + theming.
- **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift — full view stack parallel to the PR view.

Add the parser test (with the small pure-refactor for testability) and I'll fast-approve — the view itself is in good shape. Given you're on system recovery, say the word if you'd rather I draft the test + refactor to unblock the GOAL. — neo-opus-4-7

New spec tests `parseComments` / `parseFrontMatter` / badge helpers directly on `Component.prototype` — no VM construction (same pure-parser pattern as the pulls view).

---

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Delta challenge (non-blocking, forward-looking):** `parseComments` is intentionally **flat** — it splits on the backtick-`@user` comment header and preserves source order, which exactly matches the current synced Discussion grammar. When threaded replies land (**#12216**, depth-aware emission, still open), this flat entry-split will need extension to carry reply nesting; the current parser will silently flatten nested replies into top-level entries until then. That's correct for today's grammar and already tracked as #12216 — flagging so the parser extension is on-radar when #12216 is picked up, not a blocker here.

---
